name: build_phase4_orchestrator
outcome_goal: Build the LLM orchestrator that generates app manifests from user intent

jobs.label: build_manifest_generator
jobs.prompt:
You are working at the Praxis repository root. Use Postgres at $WORKFLOW_DATABASE_URL.

Build the LLM manifest generator at: Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/manifest_generator.py

This module takes a user intent + registry matches and generates an app manifest JSON that the React shell can render.

It must:

1. Class ManifestGenerator accepting a SyncPostgresConnection

2. generate(intent: str, match_result: MatchResult) -> GeneratedManifest
   - Takes the intent and matched registry items
   - Constructs a prompt for the LLM that includes:
     - The user's intent
     - Available UI components (from match_result) with their props_schema
     - Available calculations with their input/output schemas
     - Available workflows with their input schemas and trigger types
     - The app manifest JSON schema (state + layout + bindings)
   - Calls the LLM (via claude CLI: claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --print -p <prompt_file>)
   - Parses the LLM response to extract the manifest JSON
   - Validates the manifest structure
   - Returns GeneratedManifest

3. refine(manifest_id: str, user_feedback: str) -> GeneratedManifest
   - Loads existing manifest from Postgres
   - Sends the current manifest + user feedback to the LLM
   - Gets an updated manifest
   - Saves new version to app_manifest_history
   - Updates app_manifests with new version

4. GeneratedManifest dataclass:
   - manifest_id: str
   - manifest: dict (the JSON spec)
   - version: int
   - confidence: float
   - explanation: str (what the LLM decided and why)

5. The manifest JSON schema that the LLM must produce:
   {
     "state": { "key": "initial_value", ... },
     "layout": {
       "type": "SlotLayout",
       "children": [
         {
           "id": "unique_id",
           "component": "registry_component_name",
           "props": { ... },
           "bindings": {
             "eventName": {
               "action": "SET_STATE" | "EXECUTE_CALCULATION" | "EXECUTE_WORKFLOW",
               "target": "state_key" | "calculation_ref" | "workflow_ref",
               "inputs": { ... },
               "onSuccess": { "action": "SET_STATE", ... }
             }
           }
         }
       ]
     }
   }

Also add API endpoints to surfaces/api/workflow_api.py:
- POST /manifest/generate — takes {intent: str}, runs intent_matcher + manifest_generator, returns manifest
- POST /manifest/refine — takes {manifest_id: str, feedback: str}, runs refine, returns updated manifest
- GET /manifest/:id — returns a manifest by ID

Wire into MCP server too: dag_manifest_generate and dag_manifest_refine tools.

Write all the code. Verify it loads.
